free_text: Yields and recoil properties of products from the
reaction between natural copper and 86 MeV/A 12-C ions
'ISOLDE' beam line.
Unpublished.
The target stack consisted of three high purity
Al-foils 10 mu-m thick, followed by a carbon
guard foil and a copper target foil covered by two
carbon foils serving as forward and backward recoil
catchers. The copper targets were  20 mg/cm2,
approximately and the target stack had a total
thickness 150 mg/cm2. The total energy loss through
the was  30 MeV, approximately.
The values are weighed averages from one to three
bombardments
The forward to backward ratio (F/B). 'F'
and 'B' are the ratios of activity found in the
forward and backward foil to the total activity.
2*W(F+B)  'W' is the target thickness
in mg/cm2
The errors are the biggest one of either
spread among parallels or the combined errors
from single bombardments.
Uncertainty in the 2*W(F+B)
Uncertainty in the ratio
.Monte-Carlo cascade evaporation calculations.
Tbl.2 from Zeitschrift fuer Phys.,A306(1982)43
SD: Corrections in all Subents. BIB updated
The accuracy of data presentation  has been revised.
Energy units(value) changed (MEV -> MEV/A).
SD: SF5=IND/M+ and SF9=EXP deleted from
REACTION code. MISC3 -> MISC1-ERR; MISC4 -> MISC2-ERR.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
MISC3 -> MISC1-ERR; MISC4 -> MISC2-ERR. Data from
Subent 004 added.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
MISC3 -> MISC1-ERR; MISC4 -> MISC2-ERR.
